summary:
Tracheal suctioning should last less than 15 seconds to prevent hypoxia and mucosal damage. Evidence confirms that durations beyond this threshold increase risk without benefit, making option A (10–15 seconds) clinically appropriate and safe.  

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{A}}
